User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account on the PrepStack platform, storing profile information and authentication identifiers (excluding passwords).

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

username.description: The unique 10-character alphanumeric user ID used for login. (Must be unique across all users.)

email.description: The email address of the user, used for contact and account recovery.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a major learning subject or programming language module on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

title.description: The title of the course (e.g., 'Data Structures', 'Python Programming').

description.description: A brief description of what the course covers.

category.description: The category of the course (e.g., 'Computer Science', 'Programming Language', 'Aptitude').

imageUri.description: URI to an image representing the course for UI display.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents a specific section or sub-topic within a Course, containing detailed lessons and content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this module belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Module)

title.description: The title of the module (e.g., 'Module 1: Basics', 'Stack and Queue Operations').

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

order.description: The display order of the module within its parent course.

estimatedCompletionTimeMinutes.description: The estimated time in minutes required to complete this module.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a single learning unit or piece of content within a Module, such as an explanation, code example, or exercise.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N Lesson)

title.description: The title of the lesson (e.g., 'Introduction to Arrays', 'Binary Search Tree Concepts').

contentType.description: The type of content this lesson primarily presents (e.g., 'explanation', 'codeExample', 'exercise', 'miniTest').

content.description: The main content of the lesson, which can include explanations, code snippets, diagrams, rich text, or exercise instructions. Stored as markdown or HTML.

order.description: The display order of the lesson within its parent module.

Test.title: Test
Test.description: Represents a mock test or skill assessment with a defined set of questions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Test entity.

title.description: The title of the test (e.g., 'Python Skill Test', 'Quantitative Aptitude Test').

description.description: A brief description of the test's scope and content.

category.description: The category of the test (e.g., 'Programming', 'Aptitude', 'Computer Science').

durationMinutes.description: The allowed duration for the test in minutes.

numQuestions.description: The total number of questions in the test.

questionIds.description: References to Questions included in this test. (Relationship: Test N:N Question)

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question that can be part of a test, supporting various question types and dynamic generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

questionText.description: The text of the question, potentially including placeholders for dynamic values (e.g., 'If X trains travel Y km...').

options.description: An ordered list of answer options for multiple-choice questions. For numerical questions, this might be empty or contain hints.

correctOptionIndex.description: The zero-based index of the correct option in the 'options' array. For numerical questions, this might point to a specific generated value.

explanation.description: A detailed explanation of the correct answer and solution.

difficulty.description: The perceived difficulty of the question (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

TestAttempt.title: TestAttempt
TestAttempt.description: Records a user's single attempt at completing a Test, including their answers and final score.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this test attempt. (Relationship: User 1:N TestAttempt)

testId.description: Reference to the Test that was attempted. (Relationship: Test 1:N TestAttempt)

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the user started the test attempt.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed or submitted the test attempt.

score.description: The raw score obtained by the user in this attempt.

accuracyPercentage.description: The percentage of correct answers out of attempted questions.

attemptPercentage.description: The percentage of questions attempted out of the total number of questions.

correctAnswersCount.description: The total number of questions answered correctly.

wrongAnswersCount.description: The total number of questions answered incorrectly.

answers.description: An array detailing the user's answer for each question in the test.

UserCourseProgress.title: UserCourseProgress
UserCourseProgress.description: Tracks a user's overall learning progress for a specific Course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserCourseProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserCourseProgress)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course for which progress is tracked. (Relationship: Course 1:N UserCourseProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The completion percentage (0-100) of the course for the user.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user accessed content within this course.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the course, or null if not yet completed.

UserModuleProgress.title: UserModuleProgress
UserModuleProgress.description: Tracks a user's detailed progress within a specific Module, including their current lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserModuleProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose module progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserModuleProgress)

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module for which progress is tracked. (Relationship: Module 1:N UserModuleProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The completion percentage (0-100) of the module for the user.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user accessed content within this module.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the module, or null if not yet completed.

currentLessonId.description: Reference to the last Lesson the user was viewing in this module, allowing them to resume learning. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserModuleProgress)

Roadmap.title: Roadmap
Roadmap.description: Defines a structured learning path, guiding students through a sequence of courses or modules.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Roadmap entity.

title.description: The title of the roadmap (e.g., 'B.Tech Placement Roadmap', 'Programming Starter Roadmap').

description.description: A brief description of the roadmap and its objectives.

courseIds.description: An ordered list of Course IDs that constitute this roadmap. (Relationship: Roadmap 1:N Course)

targetAudience.description: The intended audience for this roadmap (e.g., 'B.Tech Students', 'Beginners', 'Non-Tech to Tech').

Resource.title: Resource
Resource.description: Represents a supplementary learning resource such as a resume template, cheat sheet, or interview guide.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resource entity.

title.description: The title of the resource (e.g., 'Modern Tech Resume Template', 'DSA Cheat Sheet').

description.description: A brief description of the resource's content or purpose.

category.description: The category of the resource (e.g., 'Resume Templates', 'Cheat Sheets', 'Interview Guides').

fileUrl.description: The URL to the actual resource file (e.g., PDF document).

fileSizeKB.description: The size of the resource file in kilobytes.

previewImageUrl.description: The URL to a preview image for the resource.

tags.description: A list of tags for classifying the resource (e.g., 'ATS', 'Fresher', 'Java').

ContactMessage.title: ContactMessage
ContactMessage.description: Stores messages submitted by users through the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactMessage entity.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the message.

email.description: The email address of the sender, for replies.

message.description: The content of the message submitted.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible only by the owning user. The `{userId}` wildcard corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

definition.description: A lookup collection to enforce the uniqueness of the custom 10-character alphanumeric `username`. The document ID is the unique username itself, and it contains the Firebase UID of the user who claimed it. This ensures authorization independence for username uniqueness checks.

params.name: usernameId
params.description: The unique 10-character alphanumeric user ID chosen by the user.

definition.description: Stores globally accessible course definitions. All documents in this collection share the same security posture (readable by authenticated users), facilitating QAPs and authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course.

definition.description: Stores globally accessible module definitions, referencing their parent `courseId`. All modules are readable by authenticated users, ensuring authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the module.

definition.description: Stores globally accessible lesson content, referencing their parent `moduleId`. All lessons are readable by authenticated users, ensuring authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lesson.

definition.description: Stores globally accessible mock test definitions, including references to questions. All tests are readable by authenticated users, supporting QAPs and authorization independence.

params.name: testId
params.description: The unique identifier for the test.

definition.description: Stores the global bank of questions used in tests. All questions are readable by authenticated users, ensuring QAPs and authorization independence.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question.

definition.description: Records a user's attempt at a test. This data is user-specific, with the `userId` implicitly denormalized into the path for authorization independence. Only the owning user can read/write their own test attempts, supporting QAPs for user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user who made the attempt.

params.name: testAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific test attempt.

definition.description: Tracks a user's overall progress for a specific course. The `{userId}` is denormalized in the path, and `{courseId}` serves as the document ID for 1:1 mapping, ensuring authorization independence and efficient QAPs for user-specific progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user whose progress is tracked.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the course.

definition.description: Tracks a user's detailed progress within a specific module, including the current lesson. The `{userId}` is denormalized in the path, and `{moduleId}` serves as the document ID, ensuring authorization independence and efficient QAPs for user-specific module progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user whose module progress is tracked.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the module.

definition.description: Defines structured learning paths. These are globally accessible content, readable by authenticated users, ensuring QAPs and authorization independence.

params.name: roadmapId
params.description: The unique identifier for the roadmap.

definition.description: Stores supplementary learning resources like templates and cheat sheets. These are globally accessible content, readable by authenticated users, ensuring QAPs and authorization independence.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the resource.

definition.description: Stores messages submitted through the contact form. These messages can be created by any authenticated user and are typically read by platform administrators. Authorization for administrators would be based on an `AdminRole` collection, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact message.

definition.description: An administrative role collection where the existence of a document with the user's UID as its ID grants administrative privileges. This design adheres to DBAC (Database Access Control) and ensures authorization independence by relying on a simple `exists()` check.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for PrepStack is designed with paramount focus on Authorization Independence, enabling robust and debuggable security rules, and efficient querying (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User-Owned Data:** For all user-specific data such as `TestAttempt`, `UserCourseProgress`, and `UserModuleProgress`, the design strictly adheres to path-based ownership. Documents are stored in subcollections under `/users/{userId}/`. For example, `/users/{userId}/testAttempts/{testAttemptId}`. This implicitly denormalizes the `userId` into the document path itself. Security rules can then directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard from the path, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` operations to check ownership in a parent document. Each document within these subcollections also explicitly contains a `userId` field for redundancy and clarity.
2.  **Global Content:** Core learning content (Courses, Modules, Lessons, Tests, Questions, Roadmaps, Resources) is stored in top-level collections (e.g., `/courses`, `/modules`). These collections are intended to be globally readable by any authenticated user. Since their access control is homogeneous and doesn't depend on attributes of other documents or user-specific permissions (beyond authentication), no denormalization for authorization is needed within these documents. This design simplifies security rules to a simple `allow read: if request.auth.uid != null;` for these collections.
3.  **Unique User ID:** The custom 10-character alphanumeric `username` (distinct from Firebase UID) is enforced via a dedicated `/usernames/{usernameId}` collection. Uniqueness is checked by attempting to create a document with the desired username as its ID and using a security rule like `allow create: if !exists(/usernames/$(usernameId))`. This ensures uniqueness atomically without `get()` dependencies on the `/users` collection.
4.  **Admin Roles:** For administrative actions (like reading `contactMessages`), an `AdminRole` is implemented as an existence-based check using `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`. This means checking `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` in rules, which is an atomic, independent check.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Global Content Listing:** Top-level collections like `/courses`, `/tests`, `/roadmaps`, `/resources` allow secure and efficient `list` operations. Security rules permit authenticated users to read all documents (`request.auth.uid != null`), ensuring that client-side queries can list all available content without being implicitly filtered by Firestore rules. The rule simply grants access to the entire collection, preventing partial data fetches or rule violations.
2.  **User-Specific Data Listing:** For collections like `/users/{userId}/testAttempts`, `list` operations are implicitly secured by the path structure. A user can only `list` documents within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/testAttempts` subcollection. Any attempt to list data for a different `userId` would be denied by the security rule `request.auth.uid == userId`, thus fulfilling the QAP principle by ensuring that `list` queries only return data the user is authorized to see in its entirety, without server-side filtering applying additional constraints.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, prevents common security pitfalls related to hierarchical dependencies, and supports the scalability requirements of a multi-user learning platform by segregating data based on its access patterns.
